skill.name: paper-context-resolver
skill.description: Optional narrow helper skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Use only when the README and repository files leave a narrow reproduction-critical gap and the task is to resolve a specific paper detail such as dataset split, preprocessing, evaluation protocol, checkpoint mapping, or runtime assumption from primary paper sources while recording conflicts. Do not use for general paper summary, repo scanning, environment setup, command execution, title-only paper lookup, or replacing README guidance by default.
skill.summary: Resolves narrow paper details that block README-first AI repository reproduction.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to descriptive text, and the backtick alert points to a markdown inline file path. No prompt injection, exfiltration behavior, command execution, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert at agents/openai.yaml:2 points to a short_description field. It contains plain skill metadata, not cryptographic code or insecure algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is YAML metadata text only. There is no hashing, encryption, key handling, or algorithm selection on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The alert at SKILL.md:46 is an inline markdown reference to references/paper-assisted-reproduction.md. It is not Ruby code or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit a documentation path in markdown. No interpreter, subprocess call, or user-controlled command string is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert at SKILL.md:3 points to the skill description. It describes research workflow boundaries and does not include cryptographic implementation details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural-language description. No evidence was found for weak cryptography or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior.

content.value_statement: AI repository reproduction often stalls when README files omit one critical paper detail. This skill narrows the question, checks primary paper sources, and records conflicts without replacing README guidance.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a README-first reproduction task needs paper context.
Focuses on dataset splits, preprocessing, evaluation protocols, checkpoints, and runtime assumptions.
Prioritizes primary paper sources such as README-linked papers, official pages, arXiv, and OpenReview.
Separates direct paper evidence from inference in the final answer.
Records conflicts between README guidance and paper details instead of silently replacing instructions.
limitations: Does not perform general paper summaries or related-work explanations.
Does not run commands, install environments, or scan repositories.
Requires a concrete reproduction question before it should be used.
Does not resolve title-only paper lookups without a specific reproduction gap.

use_cases.title: Clarify Evaluation Splits
use_cases.description: Resolve which dataset split a model should use when the README is ambiguous and the paper gives protocol details.

use_cases.title: Compare README and Paper Instructions
use_cases.description: Document where repository instructions differ from the paper before changing a reproduction plan.

use_cases.title: Map Checkpoints to Variants
use_cases.description: Use paper evidence to connect checkpoint names with model variants or runtime assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Missing Detail
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a single reproduction gap.
prompt_templates.prompt: The README does not say which dataset split to use. Check the linked paper only for that detail and note any conflict.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Preprocessing Ambiguity
prompt_templates.prompt: The repository README gives incomplete preprocessing instructions. Use the primary paper to identify reproduction-relevant preprocessing steps and separate evidence from inference.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Evaluation Protocol
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request comparing repository and paper evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the README evaluation instructions with the paper protocol for the specific benchmark. Report only reproduction-critical differences and preserve README-first guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Checkpoint Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: The checkpoint names do not clearly map to paper variants. Use primary paper sources to infer the mapping, state confidence, and record unresolved gaps.

output_examples.input: README is unclear about validation or test split for evaluation.
output_examples.output: Use the paper-linked evaluation section to identify the split. Report the exact source, note whether README agrees, and avoid broader paper summary.

output_examples.input: The repository has two checkpoints but no variant explanation.
output_examples.output: Map each checkpoint to the closest paper variant when evidence exists. Label unsupported mappings as inference and list remaining uncertainty.

output_examples.input: README preprocessing differs from the paper.
output_examples.output: Record both instructions, explain the conflict, and keep README guidance as the default unless the user asks for a documented override.

best_practices: Start with README and repository evidence before invoking the skill.
Ask one reproduction-critical question at a time.
Cite source type and distinguish direct evidence from inference.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for broad paper summaries or novelty explanations.
Replacing README instructions without documenting a conflict.
Starting from only a paper title with no concrete reproduction gap.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when README-first reproduction is blocked by one narrow paper detail.

faq.question: Can it summarize a full paper?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for reproduction-critical details, not general summaries.

faq.question: Which sources does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers README-linked papers, official project pages, official paper pages, arXiv, and OpenReview.

faq.question: Does it override README instructions?
faq.answer: No. It records conflicts and preserves README-first guidance by default.

faq.question: Can it run repository setup commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides research context and does not execute setup or evaluation commands.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the target repository, the unresolved reproduction question, existing README evidence, and any known paper links.
